metadata.name: settlement
metadata.description: Stoppage settlement: verify TxLINE proofs via CPI, mark markets settled

instructions.name: resolve_market
docs: Resolve a market by verifying a proof against an operator's
validator program on-chain.
1. CPI into `validator_program` with the caller-supplied
instruction data (complete — including its discriminator)
2. Read the return data (bool) — true means the predicate holds
3. Verify the result matches the expected outcome
4. Record the validator on the receipt and emit `MarketResolved`
Oracle-agnostic: the validator is supplied as the first remaining
account, followed by the readonly account(s) it reads (the
carriers of the anchored root). TxLINE's `validate_stat` is the
reference validator, but any program returning a bool works. This
program never learns which validator it CPI'd into beyond recording
its id — the market program binds a market to an approved oracle
and cross-checks the receipt's validator in `settle_from_proof`.
Permissionless: any keeper can call this. The proof itself is the
authority — if the validator rejects it, the CPI fails and the
entire transaction reverts.
The `validator_ix_data` is the complete instruction data for the
validator (its 8-byte discriminator + borsh-serialized args).
Building it in the SDK keeps oracle-specific serialization in
TypeScript, where the oracle types are defined.

accounts.name: resolver
docs: Permissionless keeper — anyone can call resolve_market. The
proof is the authority, not the caller.

accounts.name: market
docs: The market being resolved. Owned by programs/market.
program binds this market to an approved oracle at creation and
cross-checks the validator recorded on the receipt at settlement.

errors.name: InvalidReturnSource
errors.msg: Return data did not come from the validator program

errors.name: ProofOutcomeMismatch
errors.msg: Proof outcome does not match validator result

errors.name: InvalidOutcome
errors.msg: Resolution outcome must be YES (0) or NO (1)

errors.name: InvalidMarketOwner
errors.msg: Market is not owned by the Stoppage market program

errors.name: MissingValidatorProgram
errors.msg: No validator program supplied in remaining accounts

types.name: MarketResolved
docs: The proof-carrying resolution event. This is what the Verifiable
Resolution UI renders: the raw statement, the anchored root, the
outcome, and a hash of the Merkle path. A judge or user can
independently verify the proof against the anchored root without
trusting Stoppage.

fields.name: market
docs: The market that was resolved.

fields.name: statement
docs: The raw statement being proven, e.g. "GOAL:FRA:63:00".

fields.name: validator_program
docs: The validator program that verified the proof via CPI.

fields.name: merkle_root
docs: The Merkle root anchored on Solana (from the oracle's root PDA).

fields.name: outcome
docs: The outcome: 0=YES, 1=NO, 2=VOID.

fields.name: proof_hash
docs: Keccak-256 hash of the Merkle root — compact on-chain reference.

fields.name: resolver
docs: Who called resolve_market (permissionless — anyone).

fields.name: timestamp
docs: On-chain timestamp of resolution.

fields.name: validated_on_chain
docs: Whether the proof was verified on-chain via the validator CPI.

types.name: Resolution
docs: Immutable proof-gated receipt. The market program checks this exact PDA
before it can change a market from open to settled, and cross-checks
`validator_program` against the oracle the market was created with.

fields.name: validator_program
docs: The validator program that verified the proof via CPI. Recorded so
the market program can bind settlement to the market's oracle.
